props.title: Correlation Analysis Between the Number of Acupuncture Treatments and Embryo Outcomes in POSEIDON Criteria Poor Ovarian Response Patients

props.content: The POSEIDON criteria provide a precise stratified management framework for poor prognosis patients. Acupuncture, as a commonly used adjunctive therapy in assisted reproductive technology, has an unclear relationship between the number of treatments and embryo outcomes, particularly its variability across different POSEIDON subgroups.
To investigate the **correlation** between the number of acupuncture treatments and embryo laboratory outcome indicators in patients across different POSEIDON subgroups.

props.title: Statistical Method
props.message: Pearson correlation coefficient (r) was used to analyze the linear correlation between total acupuncture sessions and key outcomes.

props.value: Estradiol level on HCG trigger day

props.title: Note
props.message: This abstract represents only the introduction and methodology sections. Full results, discussion, and conclusions are not included in the attached document.
